task_completion.description: Verify the target directory for the Talon tools was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file explorer window, terminal output, or editor tree showing the folder 'fbi_tools' exists within 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Python module file was created in the target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the file 'fbi_search.py' visible in the 'fbi_tools' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the Talon command file was created in the target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the file 'fbi_search.talon' visible in the 'fbi_tools' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the Python script defines the required Talon action and references the correct JSON data path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the code inside 'fbi_search.py'. It must contain a Talon action definition for 'insert_fugitive_record(search_term: str)' (usually under a @mod.action_class decorator) and explicitly contain the file path 'C:\workspace\data\fbi_wanted.json'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Python script contains the correct formatting logic for both found and not found records.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the code inside 'fbi_search.py'. It must show logic to format a found record with 'SUBJECT:', 'ALIASES:', and 'WARNING:' (handling null/empty as 'None'). It must also show the fallback string 'NO RECORD FOUND FOR: [search_term]'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Talon file defines the correct voice command and calls the Python action.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the code inside 'fbi_search.talon'. It must contain the global voice command 'fugitive search <user.text>:' and show it executing 'user.insert_fugitive_record(...)' with the dictated text.

integrity.description: Ensure the files are actually saved to the disk in the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the text editor does not show an 'unsaved' indicator (like a dot or asterisk next to the filename) for fbi_search.py and fbi_search.talon, or look for terminal commands confirming the files were written.

integrity.description: Ensure the Python script contains actual JSON parsing and search logic, not just hardcoded outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python script should import 'json', open the file, iterate through the 'items' array, and check the 'title' field, rather than just returning a static string for a specific test name.

integrity.description: Ensure the Python script uses genuine Talon API imports.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for 'from talon import Module, actions' (or similar) at the top of the Python script. There should be no evidence of the agent creating fake/mock Talon modules just to bypass errors.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target directory: C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\fbi_tools. Target Python file: fbi_search.py. Target Talon file: fbi_search.talon. Target JSON data path: C:\workspace\data\fbi_wanted.json. The task requires creating these files with specific logic for searching the JSON and formatting the output.
